personal.name: Alsahid Simms
personal.summary: Software Engineer experienced in building secure, high-availability systems across the full software development lifecycle (SDLC). Background in maintaining and modernising critical infrastructure for high-security government clients (National Crime Agency) and financial institutions (Yapily). Skilled in stabilising legacy Java environments and engineering secure API layers to support digital transformation. Focused on delivering high-availability backend solutions in strictly regulated sectors.
personal.intro: A disciplined and security-minded engineer dedicated to architecting resilient, high-availability systems that power critical infrastructure from concept to production.

highlights: Member of the Core Engineering Team responsible for developing the platform's foundational services. Tasked with enforcing security and PSD2 compliance for a system serving 2,000+ financial institutions.

Regulatory Delivery (VoP): Engineered key features for the Verification of Payee service to meet the October 2025 EU Instant Payments Regulation. Implemented strict IBAN validation and Regex-based character normalisation.

Microservices Resilience: Enhanced fault tolerance for critical downstream dependencies by implementing exponential backoff to handle transient outages. Enforced an error masking strategy that maps internal failures (500) to generic dependency errors (424).
Unified Security Architecture: Contributed to the unification of access control logic across internal infrastructure and external Open Banking services using JWTs.
Scalability & Performance: Optimised core endpoints to support high-volume querying by implementing cursor-based pagination, dynamic filtering, and database indexing.

Infrastructure & CI/CD: Orchestrated continuous delivery via ArgoCD and Kubernetes. Leveraged Docker for containerisation, K8s port forwarding, and Grafana telemetry to visualise inter-service interactions.
Backend Software Engineer for the National Crime Agency’s digital evidence platform, driving the development of modern microservices and the modernisation of critical legacy systems.

Microservices & API Development: Collaborated within a 5-person team to develop digital evidence capture REST APIs, documenting contracts with Swagger. Utilised Spring Boot Actuator and extended pre-existing Spring AOP pointcuts for a granular digital evidence audit trail.

Concurrent Engineering & Quality: Context-switched seamlessly between Java 8 microservices and the legacy Java 6 monolith. Ensured robust software quality through comprehensive testing and SonarQube metrics.
Technical Leadership: Mentored two junior developers while implementing feature flags for controlled deployments and Fuzzy Matching algorithms. Led sprint demos to align stakeholder expectations.
Legacy Modernisation: Transitioned to sole backend engineer for the monolith. Managed complex database schema migrations across multiple projects and led PI planning sessions.
Contributed to the digitisation of the County Court platform for HM Courts & Tribunals Service.
Digital Transformation: Refactored legacy REST APIs and resolved bugs for the court filing system, directly supporting the transition to digital services.
Quality Assurance: Validated API reliability using Postman and maintained high test coverage standards with JaCoCo to prevent regression during the modernisation process.
Promoted from Associate to Senior Software Engineer based on delivery excellence across Government and Investment Banking sectors.

Senior Software Engineer (Jun 2021 – Sep 2021): Contributed to the development of a Function-as-a-Service solution in Node.js using AWS SAM for the DWP's SMS notification service. Led the migration of legacy XML APIs to RESTful standards.
Analyst Software Engineer (Dec 2019 – Jun 2021): Delivered full-stack microservices on GOV.UK for the HMRC National Duty Repayment Centre, integrating with PEGA workflow systems.
Associate Software Engineer (Dec 2018 – Dec 2019): Maintained critical Home Office reporting systems and managed SIT/UAT deployments, coordinating daily workflows with offshore development teams.

Associate Software Engineer (Jun 2017 – Nov 2018): Directed a testing team of 6 for a BAE Systems defence programme. Supported Java microservices for the UBS eFX trading platform and acted as integration analyst for the Manchester University ERP migration.
